rationale: The citing paper uses Cutright et al. (2013) as a key prior account for how consumer states affect distant brand-extension evaluations, specifically the control-based mechanism and rejection of low-fit extensions. It then explicitly positions its own contribution as different from that account, and in Study 4/General Discussion it measures perceived control to rule out control as the driver of its effects. This is more than a passing citation because it informs theory positioning, alternative-explanation testing, and interpretation of contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the consumer-state brand-extension literature that includes Cutright et al. (2013) by introducing life-role transition as a new determinant and proposing a different mechanism (self-concept ambiguity -> dialectical thinking). It engages Cutright et al. directly as a comparator and rules out perceived control to show a distinct pathway.
